title: Investigate and fix API key Authorization header format
description: Integration tests fail with 'invalid token' when using api_key auth. The current header is 'Authorization: token <key>' but Orpheus/Redacted may expect a different format (e.g. bare key or different prefix). Needs investigation against live API docs or trial-and-error during fixture capture.
notes: Root cause confirmed: API-key Authorization header is per-tracker. Orpheus wants 'token <key>' (Gazelle recommended); RED wants the bare key (no prefix). Fix: added api_key_prefix param to GazelleTransport (default 'token '), RedactedClient sets it to ''. Also discovered during live verification: (1) RED migrated redacted.ch -> redacted.sh (old domain returns 410); updated REDACTED_BASE_URL. (2) RED rejects requests lacking a custom User-Agent; added default UA 'python-gazelle'. (3) Orpheus omits userstats.requiredRatio -> made UserStats.required_ratio Optional. Orpheus auth VERIFIED live (user.me OK, id=REDACTED). RED still returns 401 'bad credentials' = key value issue (likely rotated key in .env), NOT a code bug; awaiting valid key to confirm.
close_reason: Fixed per-tracker API-key auth: Orpheus uses 'token <key>', RED uses bare key (api_key_prefix param). Also fixed RED domain redacted.ch->redacted.sh, added default User-Agent (RED 401s without one), made UserStats.required_ratio optional (Orpheus omits it). Both trackers verified live: Orpheus id=REDACTED, RED id=REDACTED.

title: Task 12: Integration tests
description: Set up tests/conftest.py with load_dotenv(). Integration conftest with skip-if-missing credential fixtures. Integration tests for OrpheusClient and RedactedClient against live APIs (read-only, both auth methods). Finalize __init__.py public exports. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Integration tests, public exports, and security fixes committed

title: Task 11: API fixtures and model tests
description: Write devtools/capture_fixtures.py to fetch real API responses from Orpheus and Redacted. Write fixture-driven model tests validating Pydantic models against real responses. Fix any field mismatches. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 10: Sync wrapper
description: Implement _BackgroundLoop (daemon thread + persistent event loop), _SyncProxy (__getattr__ wrapping async methods), GazelleSyncClient, OrpheusClientSync, RedactedClientSync. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 9: GazelleClient and tracker subclasses
description: Implement GazelleClient composing transport + resource properties. OrpheusClient and RedactedClient subclasses with correct base URLs. Async context manager support. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 8: Resource layer
description: Implement BaseResource and all 7 resources: TorrentResource (get/search/download), ArtistResource (get/search), RequestResource, CollageResource, UserResource, InboxResource, NotificationResource. Unit tests with StubTransport. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 7: Pydantic v2 response models
description: Implement GazelleModel base with camelCase alias_generator and extra=ignore. Implement all resource models: Torrent/TorrentGroup/TorrentResult, Artist/ArtistResult, Request/RequestResult, Collage, User/UserStats, Message, Notification. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.

title: Task 6: Transport — retry and error mapping
description: Implement exponential backoff retry for 429 and 5xx. Map status codes to typed GazelleError subclasses. Non-retryable: 400/401/403/404. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 5: Token-bucket rate limiter
description: Implement TokenBucket class using asyncio.Lock and asyncio.sleep. Wire into GazelleTransport.request(). Default 3 req/s, configurable. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 4: Transport — authentication
description: Implement cookie/session auth (login POST, session cookie) and API key auth (Authorization header) with auto re-auth on 401/403. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 3: Transport — core request and JSON parsing
description: Implement GazelleTransport with httpx.AsyncClient, base URL, Gazelle JSON response parsing, and MockTransport test helper. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 2: Error hierarchy

close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Project setup — deps and module skeleton

close_reason: Closed

title: Orpheus & Redacted tracker adapters
description: Tracker-specific adapter layer handling API quirks and endpoint signature differences between Orpheus and Redacted. Includes integration test suite validating both trackers. Part of Core API Client epic (python-gazelle-ahf).
close_reason: Verified done. OrpheusClient/RedactedClient with per-tracker URL + auth-header divergence; 4 adapter unit tests; integration suite (test_orpheus/test_redacted, 4 each); divergences via nmq + 6ue.

title: Gazelle endpoint coverage
description: Typed wrappers for all standard Gazelle endpoints: torrents, artists, requests, collages, user, inbox, notifications. Part of Core API Client epic (python-gazelle-ahf).
close_reason: Verified done. Task 7 (ecm) + Task 8 (1bj). All seven endpoint namespaces have typed wrappers: torrents, artists, requests, collages, user, inbox, notifications. Core read methods per group; exhaustive breadth can be a future issue.

title: Authentication & session management
description: Cookie/session-based auth and API key support. Handle login, session persistence, and re-auth on expiry. Part of Core API Client epic (python-gazelle-ahf).
close_reason: Verified done. Delivered via Task 4 (6dq) + bug nmq. transport.py: api_key auth, cookie login with keeplogged=1 session persistence (_login), re-auth on 401 expiry (transport.py:100-103, test_cookie_auth_reauths_on_401).

title: Rate limiting & retry logic
description: Respect per-tracker rate limits, implement exponential backoff and retry on transient errors. Part of Core API Client epic (python-gazelle-ahf).
close_reason: Verified done. Delivered via Task 6 (3z5). TokenBucket per-tracker rate limiting + exponential backoff/retry on 429/5xx (test_retries_on_429/500, test_raises_rate_limit_error_after_max_retries, test_does_not_retry_on_404).

title: [Epic] Core API Client
description: Foundation library for all Gazelle tracker interactions. Covers authentication, rate limiting, retry logic, full endpoint coverage (torrents, artists, requests, collages, user, inbox, notifications), typed response models, and tracker-specific adapters for Orpheus and Redacted. All other feature epics depend on this.
close_reason: Epic complete. All four feature areas verified and closed: 41c (auth/session), z0v (rate limit/retry), 59a (endpoint coverage), 11h (Orpheus/RED adapters). Implementation shipped via closed Task 1-12 issues + nmq/6ue; full non-integration suite 42 passed on main (4b85d0b). Downstream epics 7gj (Cross-Seed) and p53 (Upload Monitoring) depend on this but are separate, not-yet-built scope.

title: Cross-seed: synchronous surface (cross_seed_sync)
description: Add cross_seed_sync(source_sync_client, source_torrent_id, target_sync_client) in sync.py that runs the async cross_seed on the source client's background loop and returns the result without await. Plan Task 6.
close_reason: cross_seed_sync (runs async cross_seed on source client's background loop, returns without await) + export added (commit 9301ad2). 144 passed, basedpyright 0/0/0.

title: Cross-seed: integration test, README, quality gate
description: Opt-in cross-tracker live integration test (read-only, max_retries=0, skips unless BOTH tracker keys + a source torrent id are set). README cross-seed usage example. Full quality gate: pytest --ignore=tests/integration, ruff check+format, basedpyright (whole project), codespell. Plan Task 8.
close_reason: Opt-in live cross-seed integration test (fixture-based skip, read-only, both-tracker-keys + source-id gated), README usage example, and full quality gate all clean: 144 passed/31 skipped, ruff+format+codespell clean, basedpyright 0/0/0 whole project (commit f1a0405).

title: Cross-seed: CrossSeedResult model + test harness
description: Add the frozen CrossSeedResult dataclass (match, torrent_file bytes, source/target torrent ids, confidence='exact') in new src/pygazelle/crossseed.py, and the cross-seed test fakes in tests/support.py (CrossSeedTransport + make_torrent_payload/make_browse_group/make_browse_row). Foundational for the verify/find/orchestrator tasks. Plan Tasks 1-2.
close_reason: CrossSeedResult dataclass + module skeleton + cross-seed test fakes (CrossSeedTransport + payload builders) added (commits 0e1f92e, 6d0b7f1). Payload round-trips through Torrent; 129 passed, basedpyright clean. Note: forward-use imports carry temporary noqa/pyright-ignore until find_candidates uses them.

title: Cross-seed: cross_seed orchestrator + public exports
description: Implement async cross_seed(source_client, source_torrent_id, target_client, *, max_deep_checks): get source -> find_candidates -> first verify_match -> download -> CrossSeedResult; None when source has no file list or no candidate matches. Export cross_seed/find_candidates/verify_match/CrossSeedResult (and cross_seed_sync) from pygazelle.__init__. Plan Tasks 5,7.
close_reason: cross_seed orchestrator (get source -> find_candidates -> first verify_match -> download -> CrossSeedResult; None on no-filelist/no-match) + async exports (cross_seed/find_candidates/verify_match/CrossSeedResult) added (commit 209e156). 142 passed, basedpyright 0/0/0, import clean.

title: Monitoring integration test, README example, quality gate
description: Opt-in live read-only user_torrents integration test (max_retries=0, skips without creds). README monitoring usage example. Full quality gate: pytest --ignore=tests/integration, ruff check + format, basedpyright, codespell. Plan Tasks 11-12.
close_reason: Opt-in live integration test (ae00f6e), README example (460f8e5), and full quality gate all clean: basedpyright 0/0/0 full project, 126 passed/31 skipped, ruff+format+codespell clean (fixes in 1e192a3).

title: client.monitor() factory + public exports
description: Add GazelleClient.monitor(sources, page_size) factory returning a TorrentMonitor bound to the client. Export TorrentMonitor + TorrentChangeEvent from pygazelle.__init__ (and models from pygazelle.models). Verify no import cycle (client->monitoring with TYPE_CHECKING back-ref). Plan Tasks 8,10.
close_reason: client.monitor() factory + top-level TorrentMonitor/TorrentChangeEvent exports added (commits f3b7051, df694a9). reportImportCycles disabled (documented; inherent client<->monitor cycle). 125 passed, basedpyright clean.

title: Synchronous monitor surface
description: Add GazelleSyncClient.monitor(...) returning a _SyncProxy over the async monitor: async poll() runs on the background loop, sync dump_state/load_state pass through. Plan Task 9.
close_reason: Sync monitor surface (GazelleSyncClient.monitor() -> _SyncProxy) added in commit 36bdb9a. 126 passed, basedpyright clean.

title: Monitor state serialization (dump_state/load_state)
description: Add dump_state()/load_state() on TorrentMonitor: json-serializable snapshot (None before first poll), restored snapshot treated as baseline so only later changes report. Round-trip test. Plan Task 7.
close_reason: dump_state/load_state round-trip tests added (commit 7740a0a); 11 monitor tests pass.

title: user_torrents fixture capture + assumption-B spike
description: Extend devtools/capture_fixtures.py to capture user_torrents (uploaded+snatched) for Orpheus+Redacted. Manually capture (creds-gated) and record: per-tracker field names vs the shipped UserTorrent model, and whether deleted/trumped torrents drop off the list (assumption B). If they linger -> Task 5b required; if they drop off -> not needed. Also confirm the shipped endpoint meets monitor needs. Plan Tasks 1-2.
close_reason: Capture tooling for user_torrents shipped (commit 1c80d4b). Live capture + assumption-B verification deferred (no tracker creds); recorded in tasks.md 1.3, gating issue 2xv deferred.

title: torrent: add edition/remaster fields + description + log detail
description:
Torrent model (action=torrent) drops edition/remaster info needed to distinguish editions. Add to Torrent (all Optional):
- remaster_year (remasterYear), remaster_title (remasterTitle), remaster_record_label (remasterRecordLabel), remaster_catalogue_number (remasterCatalogueNumber)
- description (release description)
- log_checksum (logChecksum), log_count (logCount), rip_log_ids (ripLogIds: list[int])
- free_reason (freeReason, Orpheus), reported (bool)
TDD against tests/fixtures/orpheus/torrent.json (real fixture). Tracker-divergence: RED may omit some (e.g. freeReason); keep Optional.
close_reason: Added edition/remaster fields to Torrent (remaster_year/title/record_label/catalogue_number), description, log detail (log_checksum, log_count, rip_log_ids), free_reason, reported — all Optional. log_checksum/log_count/free_reason Orpheus-only (RED omits -> None). TDD vs both fixtures. Reviewed clean. 103 tests pass, gate clean.

title: artist: enrich Artist with discography, statistics, similar_artists
description:
Artist model (action=artist) drops the most valuable parts of the response. Currently exposes only id/name/body/image/tags. Add:
- discography: the artist's torrent groups. NOTE different shape than action=torrentgroup — uses groupId/groupName/groupYear, tags as a {tagId: name} map, "torrent" (singular key) list with hasFile, extendedArtists. Needs a dedicated model (e.g. ArtistTorrentGroup), do NOT reuse models.torrents.TorrentGroup. Source key in response: "torrentgroup".
- statistics: {numGroups, numTorrents, numSeeders, numLeechers, numSnatches, numRequests} -> ArtistStatistics submodel.
- similar_artists: list of {artistId, name, score, similarId}.
Lower-priority same-endpoint extras (optional, can fold in): vanity_house, body_bbcode, has_bookmarked, notifications_enabled, requests.
All Optional/tolerant per tracker-divergence convention. TDD against tests/fixtures/orpheus/artist.json (real fixture exists). Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md
close_reason: Enriched Artist with statistics (ArtistStatistics), similar_artists (ArtistSimilar — distinct from SimilarArtist), discography (ArtistTorrentGroup/DiscographyTorrent/DiscographyArtist, alias 'torrentgroup'), plus vanity_house/body_bbcode. Dedicated discography models due to shape divergence (groupId/groupName, tags map, singular 'torrent', groupCategoryID casing). All Optional/tolerant. TDD vs orpheus/artist.json. Reviewed: one low-sev note (discography tags union unverified on RED) tracked under fixture-capture follow-up. 100 tests pass, gate clean.

title: torrents: add get_group() for torrentgroup action
description: Add TorrentResource.get_group(group_id) calling ajax.php action=torrentgroup, returning a TorrentGroup with all editions/torrents. Highest-value gap: complements existing torrents.get() (action=torrent), which only returns a single torrent with the group embedded. Add/extend TorrentGroup model + fixtures for both trackers.
close_reason: Implemented TorrentResource.get_group() for action=torrentgroup; enriched TorrentGroup (editions list + group fields) and fixed artists-from-musicInfo. Code-reviewed (2 fixes applied), 54 tests pass, ruff/basedpyright/codespell clean.

title: [Epic] API surface completeness — remaining Gazelle ajax routes
description:
The Core API Client epic (python-gazelle-ahf, closed) shipped a deliberate read-only subset: index, torrent, browse, artist, request(s), collage, inbox, notifications, download (~11 actions, ~38% of the documented surface). This epic tracks the remaining documented ajax.php actions from the OPSnet fork (the codebase Orpheus and RED descend from; docs: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md).

Missing READ actions: torrentgroup, user, usersearch, user_torrents, top10, bookmarks, subscriptions, forum (main/viewforum/viewthread), similar_artists, announcements, better, logchecker.

Missing WRITE actions: add_tag, add_log, request_fill/requestfill. (upload is owned by Cross-Upload epic python-gazelle-xn1, not duplicated here.)

Per-tracker caveat: action sets diverge slightly between Orpheus and RED; logchecker/better are RED-flavored tooling. Verify each against live fixtures from both trackers where possible.

title: Set up MkDocs + Material + mkdocstrings documentation site
description: Add a rendered documentation site for the library using MkDocs, Material theme, and mkdocstrings (Python handler) to auto-generate API reference from type hints + docstrings. Includes docs dependency group, mkdocs.yml, reference pages, and a GitHub Pages CI workflow.
close_reason: MkDocs+Material+mkdocstrings site implemented, builds clean in --strict, Pages deploy workflow added. Merged to main.

title: Capture real API response fixtures from Orpheus and Redacted
description: Run devtools/capture_fixtures.py with a valid .env to populate tests/fixtures/orpheus/ and tests/fixtures/redacted/. Blocked on fixing the API key header format (see related bug). Fixtures are required for model tests (4.3/4.4) and will reveal tracker-specific schema divergences needed for task 4.2.
close_reason: Fixtures captured locally for both trackers (index, notifications, torrent, artist x2). Kept out of git by design (tests/fixtures/ gitignored — index responses contain passkey/authkey). capture_fixtures.py fixed for per-tracker auth + artist extraction. Divergences discovered and recorded on python-gazelle-6ue; the two failing Orpheus model tests are xfail pending 6ue.

title: Cross-seed: strict file-list verification (verify_match)
description: Implement verify_match(source, candidate): strict match = identical top-level file_path AND identical sorted (path,size) list; empty file lists never match. The .torrent retrieval itself is the existing download endpoint, wired in the orchestrator. Plan Task 3.
close_reason: verify_match (strict: identical file_path + sorted (path,size) list; empty never matches) added (commit 79817e7). 5 verify tests + full crossseed file pass; basedpyright clean.

title: Cross-seed: metadata candidate discovery (find_candidates)
description: Implement find_candidates(source, target_client): search the target tracker by the source release's artist+album (groupname fallback when artist search is empty), cheaply pre-filter candidate BrowseTorrent rows on format/encoding/media/size/file_count BEFORE any per-candidate get, then fetch full file lists for survivors bounded by a deep-check cap (log truncation). Plan Task 4.
close_reason: find_candidates (search by artist+album, groupname fallback, cheap pre-filter on format/encoding/media/size/file_count before any get, deep-check cap with log, skip NotFound) added (commit 409337d). Also fixed 11 pre-existing test-file basedpyright errors. 137 passed, basedpyright 0/0/0 whole project.

title: Monitoring event & snapshot models
description: Add Pydantic models for the monitor: MonitoredTorrent, MonitorSnapshot (per-source map, json round-trip), and TorrentChangeEvent (kind deleted/trumped/removed, source, torrent_id, group_id, name from UserTorrent.name, optional replacement_torrent_id). Callback delivery is DROPPED per the design decision — the stateless event MODEL remains, callbacks do not. Plan Task 3.
close_reason: Monitoring models (MonitoredTorrent, MonitorSnapshot, TorrentChangeEvent) added in commit 824dec2. Tests pass, basedpyright clean.

title: TorrentMonitor core: snapshot-diff polling + classification
description: Implement TorrentMonitor (src/pygazelle/monitoring.py): resolve user id via me() once; fetch configured sources (uploaded/snatched) through the shipped user_torrents endpoint with pagination; diff torrent ids vs prior snapshot; classify each disappearance with ONE targeted get_group() lookup (deleted/trumped/removed); atomic snapshot commit; source restriction. Rate-limit-safe: lookups bounded by removals, never per-torrent polling. Plan Tasks 4-6.
close_reason: TorrentMonitor core (snapshot-diff, classification, pagination, atomic commit, source restriction) + MonitorTransport fake added in commits a05415d, 3e98d58, f15a24a. 9 monitor tests pass, full suite 120 passed/31 skipped, basedpyright clean.

title: [Epic] Cross-Seed
description: Cross-seed: from a source torrent id on an origin client and a target client, find the same release on the target tracker via API metadata match + strict file-list verification, and return that tracker's .torrent (bytes) ready to add to a client. API-only (no filesystem, no .torrent generation). Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-04-cross-seed.md. OpenSpec change: cross-seed.
close_reason: Cross-Seed shipped and merged to main (cross_seed orchestrator + find_candidates + verify_match + CrossSeedResult, async + sync surfaces, exports). All sub-tasks cbd/xx0/hs3/jjl/3pk/ryg closed; no deferred work. Unblocks the Cross-Upload epic (xg5).

title: [Epic] Upload Monitoring
description: Detect when the current user's uploaded AND snatched torrents are deleted or trumped on Orpheus/Redacted. Stateless TorrentMonitor.poll() returns typed change events via snapshot diffing; caller owns cadence and persistence. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-04-upload-snatch-monitoring.md. OpenSpec change: upload-snatch-monitoring. NOTE: the user_torrents endpoint already shipped (commit 0f39d3b); it is no longer part of this work.
close_reason: Core upload & snatch monitoring shipped and merged to main (TorrentMonitor: snapshot-diff detection, classification, atomic commit, dump/load state; async + sync; exports). Sub-tasks 1fm/6nh/674/6q0/q7a/p43/xev closed. python-gazelle-2xv (conditional deletion-recheck, creds-gated spike) decoupled and left as a standalone deferred follow-up.

title: Targeted deletion recheck (conditional on spike)
description: CONDITIONAL: only if the spike finds deleted torrents LINGER in user_torrents. Add a bounded per-removal torrents.get(torrent_id) recheck; classify GazelleNotFoundError as deleted. Skip entirely if deleted torrents drop off the list. Plan Task 5b.

title: openspec: reconcile main specs with shipped API surface (catch-up)
description:
The OpenSpec main specs drifted behind the implemented surface because the lightweight endpoint-addition workflow intentionally skips per-endpoint delta specs. Reconciled via a full opsx catch-up: authored change api-surface-expansion with delta specs (gazelle-client, gazelle-transport, response-models) and archived it to fold into main specs.

Folded: gazelle-client (+3 ADDED: Request resource, Personal-list resources, Site resource; ~4 MODIFIED: resource surface namespaces, torrent methods incl get_group/add_tag/add_log, artist similar, user get/search/torrents). gazelle-transport (+2 ADDED: write request path, non-idempotent write safety). response-models (-1 REMOVED tracker-specific subclasses; +2 ADDED tolerant base models + endpoint coverage). Also corrected Purpose prose in 3 specs (response-models no longer claims subclasses; gazelle-client/transport mention new namespaces + write path). All 4 specs validate. upload-snatch-monitoring change left untouched per decision.
close_reason: Done. Authored + archived change api-surface-expansion; main specs (gazelle-client/transport/response-models) now match shipped behavior, all 4 validate. 136 tests unaffected.

title: browse: enrich BrowseTorrent + TorrentResult (per-edition stats, freeleech flags)
description:
Browse result models (action=browse) are very thin. Enrich:
- BrowseTorrent: add edition_id (editionId), artists, remaster_year/title/record_label/catalogue_number, has_log (hasLog), log_score (logScore), has_cue (hasCue), scene, vanity_house, time, per-edition snatches/seeders/leechers, and freeleech flags is_freeleech/is_neutral_leech/is_personal_freeleech/can_use_token/has_snatched.
- TorrentResult (group row): add cover, bookmarked, vanity_house, group_time (groupTime), release_type (string, e.g. "Album").
All Optional/tolerant. TDD against tests/fixtures/orpheus/browse.json + redacted/browse.json. Browse is a hot path.
close_reason: Enriched BrowseTorrent (edition_id, artists, remaster_*, has_log/log_score/has_cue, scene, vanity_house, time, seeders/leechers/snatches, freeleech flags) and TorrentResult (cover, bookmarked, vanity_house, release_type str, group_time raw). All Optional. Handled divergences (groupId str/int, releaseType str, groupTime datetime/epoch). TDD vs both fixtures. Reviewed clean. 111 tests pass, gate clean.

title: torrentgroup: add releaseTypeName (+ group-block extras)
description: TorrentGroup exposes release_type as an int code but drops releaseTypeName ("Album"/"EP"/"Single"...). Add release_type_name: str | None (alias releaseTypeName) so callers don't have to map the int themselves. Also consider group-block extras: proxy_image (proxyImage), wiki_bbcode (wikiBBcode), is_bookmarked (isBookmarked), time. Cheap win. TDD against tests/fixtures/orpheus/torrent.json (group block). Optional/tolerant.
close_reason: Added TorrentGroup.release_type_name (Orpheus-only), proxy_image (Orpheus-only), is_bookmarked, time, wiki_bbcode (AliasChoices wikiBBcode/bbBody — Orpheus vs RED key divergence). All Optional. TDD vs both fixtures. Reviewed clean. 106 tests pass, gate clean.

title: collage: expose contained torrent groups (torrentgroups list)
description:
Collage model currently exposes only id/name/description/tags/num_torrents and drops the actual torrent groups the collage contains. action=collage returns a "torrentgroups" array (each entry shaped like action=torrentgroup), which maps onto the existing TorrentGroup model.

Add: torrent_groups: list[TorrentGroup] = [] (explicit alias "torrentgroups" — to_camel would produce "torrentGroups", but the API sends all-lowercase "torrentgroups"). Reuse models.torrents.TorrentGroup; no new group model.

TDD: inline test validating a documented-shape collage payload parses its torrentgroups into TorrentGroup instances (with nested torrents), and that an absent key defaults to [].

CAVEAT: no collage fixture exists locally (tests/fixtures has only artist/browse/index/notifications/torrent). Modeled from the OPSnet API doc. Follow-up: capture a real collage fixture to verify the "torrentgroups" key + casing and to model the collage metadata scalars (creatorID, collageCategoryID/Name, subscriberCount, maxGroups, maxGroupsPerUser, locked, deleted, hasBookmarked, torrentGroupIDList, description_raw) whose casing can't be trusted from the doc summary. Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md
close_reason: Added Collage.torrent_groups (list[TorrentGroup], alias 'torrentgroups'), reusing TorrentGroup. action=collage's contained groups (+ their nested torrents) now parse. TDD inline (no collage fixture exists). Modeled from OPSnet API doc; follow-up to capture a fixture + model collage metadata scalars. Reviewed clean. 95 tests pass, gate clean.

title: user: surface freeleech tokens + bonus points in UserStats
description:
The index (me()) userstats block returns more than UserStats currently models; extra="ignore" silently drops them.

Orpheus userstats includes: tokens (freeleech token count), bonusPoints, bonusPointsPerHour, class — in addition to the already-modeled uploaded/downloaded/ratio/requiredratio. RED index userstats omits tokens/bonusPoints/bonusPointsPerHour (confirmed in tests/fixtures/redacted/index.json — block stops at class). Account owner also confirms RED surfaces no token count (they hold no tokens).

Add to UserStats as Optional (tracker-divergence convention — tolerant base model, no subclasses):
- tokens: int | None
- bonus_points: int | None (alias bonusPoints)
- bonus_points_per_hour: float | None (alias bonusPointsPerHour)
- user_class: str | None (alias "class" — Python keyword, needs explicit alias)

TDD: model test asserting Orpheus full block parses (tokens == 5 per fixture) and RED slimmer block still validates (tokens is None).

Follow-up (separate, optional): investigate whether RED exposes FL token count via a different endpoint (action=user profile or dedicated). Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md
close_reason: Added Optional tokens (freeleech token count), bonus_points, bonus_points_per_hour, user_class (alias 'class') to UserStats. Orpheus-only in index userstats; RED omits them so they stay None (tracker-divergence convention). TDD: fixture-driven Orpheus/RED tests + a fixture-free CI guard. Code-reviewed clean. 92 tests pass, ruff/basedpyright/codespell clean.

title: transport: write path (POST + auth token/CSRF + write-error mapping)
description: Foundational transport capability for all mutation endpoints. Today GazelleTransport.request() is GET-only and the only POST is _login() (cookie auth) — there is no generic write path. Add: a POST-based request method (form/multipart bodies), auth token / CSRF (auth_key) handling required by write actions, and write-specific error mapping. Per-tracker divergence likely (Orpheus vs RED token mechanics) — verify against both. This is a prerequisite shared by write endpoints (python-gazelle-5m0) and cross-upload submission (python-gazelle-xn1); neither should reimplement it. Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md
design:
SAFETY: writes are non-idempotent — do NOT reuse the GET retry policy.

The GET request() retries on 429/5xx (transport.py:115-123). For a write POST that is dangerous: a write that SUCCEEDED server-side but returned 5xx or timed out would be retried and double-apply (double add_tag, double request_fill). So the write path MUST NOT blind-retry non-idempotent POSTs:
- Default max_retries = 0 for writes, OR
- retry ONLY on errors where the request provably never landed (connection refused / pre-send failure), NEVER after the body may have been processed server-side.
This makes writes safe in production, not just in tests.

TESTING: build + test the entire write path against the mocked httpx client (the _http_client injection the read path and _login already use) — assert POST body, auth token, headers, error mapping, and the no-retry behavior. Zero live calls, zero ban risk. NO live integration tests that mutate tracker state (the downstream 5m0 actions — add_tag/add_log/request_fill — are not safely reversible). Any future live smoke test would be add_tag only, opt-in via env flag, single-shot, max_retries=0 — out of scope for 9l5.

SCOPE: 9l5 is transport plumbing only (generic request_write/POST + authkey/CSRF injection + write-error mapping); it calls no mutating action itself. authkey comes from the index/_login response (already parsed) — cache + inject as a POST param. Per-tracker token mechanics (Orpheus vs RED) verified against the OPSnet API doc.
close_reason: Added GazelleTransport.request_write (POST + form/multipart, authkey fetched-from-index/cached/injected as 'auth', caller override/opt-out). Write-safety: NO retry on 429/5xx (raised immediately); single re-auth only on cookie-mode 401 (403 falls through, no resend). Transport-only — not in SupportsTransport Protocol until a resource consumes it (5m0). Mock-tested, zero live writes. Code-reviewed (narrowed 401-only). 120 tests pass, gate clean.

title: write endpoints: add_tag, add_log, request_fill
description: Add mutation endpoints: add_tag (tag a torrent group), add_log (attach rip log to a torrent), request_fill/requestfill (mark request filled). Builds on the transport write path (python-gazelle-9l5, blocking prerequisite) — do NOT reimplement POST/CSRF handling here. NOTE: action=upload is intentionally excluded — owned by Cross-Upload epic python-gazelle-xn1.
close_reason: Added write endpoints: TorrentResource.add_tag (action=add_tag) -> TagAddition, TorrentResource.add_log (action=add_log, id query param + logfiles[] multipart) -> LogAddition, RequestResource.fill (action=request_fill) -> RequestFill. New write-response models. Extended request_write with params kwarg + added it to SupportsTransport Protocol (stubs updated). All mock-tested, zero live writes. Code-reviewed: added empty-input ValueError guards. 130 tests pass, gate clean. upload intentionally excluded (xn1).

title: forum: ForumResource (main, viewforum, viewthread)
description: New ForumResource covering the forum read actions: list categories (action=forum&type=main), threads in a forum (type=viewforum, paginated), and posts in a thread incl. polls (type=viewthread). New models for Category/Forum/Thread/Post. Larger than a single method — feature-sized.
close_reason: WON'T DO (scope decision, not 'done'). Forum read endpoints (action=forum main/viewforum/viewthread) are intentionally excluded from this client. Rationale: a Gazelle API client should cover torrent/music/account data, not forum browsing/interaction — forum reading is out of the library's intended surface. No ForumResource or forum models will be added. If revisited, re-open or file a fresh issue with explicit justification.

title: site reads: top10 + announcements
description: Add site-level read endpoints. top10 (action=top10; ranked torrents/tags/users, param for type+limit) and announcements (action=announcements; site news/blog posts). New resource (e.g. SiteResource) + models.
close_reason: Implemented client.site (SiteResource.top10(type, limit) action=top10; announcements() action=announcements) + models (Top10Category, Announcements/Announcement/BlogPost), wired into async + sync clients. Code-reviewed: made Announcement.news_id/BlogPost.blog_id Optional per tracker-divergence convention. 89 tests pass, ruff/basedpyright/codespell clean.

title: artists: add similar() for similar_artists action
description: Add ArtistResource.similar(artist_id) calling ajax.php action=similar_artists, returning related artists. Small extension to the existing ArtistResource; reuse ArtistResult-style model.
close_reason: Implemented ArtistResource.similar() for action=similar_artists (+ SimilarArtist model). Code-reviewed (null-guard fix applied), 60 tests pass, lint clean.

title: personal lists: bookmarks + subscriptions
description: Add read access to the user's saved items. bookmarks (action=bookmarks, type=torrents|artists) and subscriptions (action=subscriptions, subscribed forum threads with unread status). New resource(s) + models. Both are auth'd, current-user endpoints.
close_reason: Added client.bookmarks (torrents/artists) and client.subscriptions (list) resources + models, wired into async + sync clients. Code-reviewed (release_type fix, missing fields added, _parse_list helper extracted; tag_list-as-string refuted). 79 tests pass, lint clean.

title: user: profile-by-id, usersearch, user_torrents
description: Extend UserResource beyond me()/index. Add: get(user_id) -> User (action=user, public profile + community stats); search(query) (action=usersearch); torrents(user_id, type) (action=user_torrents). Reuse/extend the User model; profile-by-id omits private fields present in index.
close_reason: Implemented UserResource.get()/search()/torrents() (actions user/usersearch/user_torrents) + models. Code-reviewed (Literal type, _params helper, field-name collision fix applied; several findings refuted with reasons). 68 tests pass, lint clean.

title: Fix pre-existing ruff lint + format violations repo-wide
description:
Running 'uv run ruff check .' reports 24 errors and 'uv run ruff format --check .' wants to reformat 9 files. These are pre-existing (predate python-gazelle-6ue) and live in files outside that change, so they were left untouched. This task clears them so the repo passes the lint/format gate documented in CLAUDE.md.

Lint (24 errors, all auto-fixable via 'ruff check --fix'):
  - 19x I001 (import block unsorted/unformatted)
  - 5x F401 (unused import)
Affected: src/pygazelle/__init__.py, client.py, resources/{artists,base,collages,inbox,notifications,requests,torrents,user}.py, tests/integration/{conftest,test_orpheus,test_redacted}.py, tests/models/conftest.py, tests/{test_client,test_errors,test_sync,test_transport}.py

Format (9 files via 'ruff format'): devtools/capture_fixtures.py, src/pygazelle/{__init__,client,sync}.py, src/pygazelle/models/__init__.py, src/pygazelle/resources/__init__.py, tests/{test_client,test_sync,test_transport}.py

Caution: before blindly applying F401 auto-fix, confirm the 5 unused imports are not intended public re-exports (check __all__ in any affected __init__.py). Run the full non-integration suite after fixing to confirm no regressions.
acceptance_criteria: 'uv run ruff check .' reports 0 errors; 'uv run ruff format --check .' reports all files formatted; 'uv run pytest --ignore=tests/integration' still passes (currently 42); no intended public re-export removed.

title: Write real README replacing simple-modern-uv template placeholder
description: README.md was still the default template stub. Replace with real docs: intro/features, install, async quick-start, sync usage, authentication, resources table, rate limiting & retries, error hierarchy, and links to CLAUDE.md and docs/. Examples verified against the actual public API.
close_reason: Replaced template placeholder README with real library docs (usage, auth, resources, errors); examples verified against the public API.

title: Document build/test, architecture, conventions, and fixture/.env setup in CLAUDE.md
description: CLAUDE.md had empty placeholder sections. Fill Build & Test (uv/pytest/ruff/basedpyright/codespell commands + .env and capture_fixtures.py setup for skipping integration/model tests), Architecture Overview (transport/client/resources/models/sync/errors layers), and Conventions & Patterns (GazelleModel camelCase aliasing, adding endpoints, tracker divergences, style, live-API care).
close_reason: Filled Build & Test, Architecture Overview, Conventions & Patterns, and added fixture/.env setup guidance in CLAUDE.md.

title: Implement tracker-specific model subclasses for Orpheus/Redacted divergences
description: Spec requires tracker-specific Pydantic model subclasses where Orpheus and Redacted return divergent schemas. Cannot be implemented until real API fixtures are captured and schema differences are known. Blocked on fixture capture task. Once fixtures are available, identify divergent fields and introduce tracker-specific submodels.
notes: Implemented on branch worktree-pygazelle-6ue-tracker-divergences (commit be39740). Made base Torrent/Artist tolerant rather than adding subclasses — see rationale. Model tests now 5 passed (was 3 passed + 2 xfailed). Quality gates pass for changed files (ruff, basedpyright 0 errors, codespell). NOT yet merged; pending code review + finish-branch.
close_reason: Implemented: base Torrent/Artist models made tolerant of Orpheus/RED divergences (commits be39740, 4b85d0b). Merged to main. Model tests 5 passed.

title: Cross-upload: metadata schema mapping
description: Translate torrent group metadata between Orpheus and Redacted schemas (artist, album, year, tags, format, media, description). Part of Cross-Upload epic (python-gazelle-xg5).

title: Cross-upload: upload submission & duplicate detection
description: Submit mapped metadata via target tracker upload API. Check for existing releases on target tracker before uploading to avoid duplicates. Part of Cross-Upload epic (python-gazelle-xg5).

title: [Epic] Cross-Upload
description: Upload a release from one tracker (Orpheus/Redacted) to the other with correct metadata. Covers metadata schema mapping, upload form submission via API, and duplicate detection before uploading. Depends on Cross-Seed epic.

title: pagination: expose currentPage/pages/numNew on list endpoints (consistent)
description:
Cross-cutting: list-returning resource methods drop the pagination metadata the API returns alongside results. Split from python-gazelle-rfb (which delivered the musicInfo credits part).

Affected (all return a bare list and drop meta):
- NotificationResource.list -> drops currentPage/pages/numNew (action=notifications)
- TorrentResource.search (action=browse) -> drops currentPage/pages
- RequestResource.search (action=requests) -> likely drops currentPage/pages

Decide a CONSISTENT approach across all of them (do not do notifications alone — that was the rfb trap). Options: (a) a generic Page[T] / Paginated container model with .results + current_page/pages/(num_new) returned by these methods (mildly breaking — changes return types; pre-1.0 so acceptable), or (b) keep list returns and add parallel *_page() methods, or (c) attach meta some other way. Pick one and apply uniformly. Tolerant/Optional per divergence convention. TDD against existing fixtures (notifications.json has currentPage/pages/numNew; browse.json has currentPage/pages). Cover Orpheus + RED.

title: torrentgroup: expose collage memberships (collages + personalCollages)
description:
The action=torrent / action=torrentgroup group block carries the torrent group's collage memberships, which TorrentGroup currently drops (extra="ignore"). Observed in tests/fixtures/redacted/torrent.json group block:
- "collages": [{ "id", "name", "numTorrents" }]
- "personalCollages": [{ "id", "name", "numTorrents" }]

Add to TorrentGroup (Optional/tolerant): collages: list[Collage] and personal_collages: list[Collage] (alias personalCollages). The entries match the existing models.collages.Collage shape (id/name/num_torrents) — reuse it. NOTE potential import-direction concern: collages.py already imports torrents.TorrentGroup (for Collage.torrent_groups), so importing Collage into torrents.py would create a circular import. Resolve by either (a) a shared/light CollageRef model, (b) a forward ref + model_rebuild, or (c) moving the shared model. Decide during implementation.

Divergence: confirmed present on RED group block; verify Orpheus (the orpheus/torrent.json group block did not show collages — may be empty/absent there). Keep Optional. TDD against tests/fixtures/redacted/torrent.json. Discovered during python-gazelle-5gx (browse enrichment) / d5f. Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md
close_reason: Added TorrentGroup.collages + personal_collages (list[CollageRef]). CollageRef is a lightweight id/name/num_torrents model in torrents.py — avoids the circular import (collages imports TorrentGroup). RED group block carries these; Orpheus omits -> []. TDD vs RED fixture. Reviewed clean. 133 tests pass, gate clean.

title: models: low-priority dropped fields (musicInfo credits, notifications meta, covers)
description:
Lower-priority dropped fields surfaced in the 2026-06 fixture audit (do as one cleanup task or split):
- Torrent group block musicInfo credits: with/remixedBy/composers/conductor/dj/producer/arranger (we only lift artists). Model as Optional credit lists.
- cover/proxyImage image URLs where not already added.
- Notifications pagination meta: action=notifications returns currentPage/pages/numNew alongside results; NotificationResource currently returns a bare list and drops them. Consider a paginated container or expose meta.
All Optional/tolerant. TDD against existing fixtures.
close_reason: Delivered the musicInfo credits piece: MusicInfo model (artists/with_/remixed_by/composers/conductor/dj/producer/arranger) on TorrentGroup.music_info, coexisting with the top-level artists lift. cover/proxyImage already covered by d5f (proxy_image) + 5gx (cover). Notifications pagination meta SPLIT to python-gazelle-rv5 (cross-cutting: browse/requests/notifications all drop it; doing one alone would be inconsistent + mildly breaking). TDD vs both fixtures. Reviewed clean. 136 tests pass, gate clean.

title: lint: exclude tests/fixtures from codespell
description: devtools/lint.py runs 'codespell src tests devtools', which scans locally-captured tests/fixtures/*.json (real tracker data, gitignored, regenerated per-dev). Real metadata trips codespell (e.g. 'Vai', 'NCE'), so 'make lint'/lint.py --check fails locally for anyone who has captured fixtures. CI is unaffected (fixtures absent). Fix: add skip for tests/fixtures in [tool.codespell] in pyproject.toml (e.g. skip = 'tests/fixtures'). Discovered while implementing python-gazelle-20t.
close_reason: Added skip='*/fixtures/*' to [tool.codespell]; codespell + lint.py --check now pass locally with captured fixtures present.

title: tooling reads: better + logchecker (RED-flavored)
description: Add the tooling read endpoints: better (action=better; single-seeded/transcode-candidate torrents) and logchecker (action=logchecker; validate ripping log quality). These are RED-flavored and may be absent/divergent on Orpheus — gate or mark Optional per the tracker-divergence convention. Lower priority/niche.

title: Commit sanitized fixtures so model tests run in CI
description: tests/fixtures/ is gitignored because real index.json responses contain passkey/authkey. As a result the model tests (tests/models/) skip in CI where no fixtures exist. Provide sanitized fixtures (redact passkey, authkey, announce URLs, and any account identifiers) that can be safely committed, so model parsing tests run in CI. Source data captured locally via devtools/capture_fixtures.py. Relates to python-gazelle-6ue (which fixes the model divergences those tests assert).

value: Workflow preference: for small, well-specified endpoint-addition tasks under epic python-gazelle-mca (e.g. 20t get_group, f54 similar_artists), the user wants direct implementation — skip the formal /superpowers:brainstorming Q&A (they rejected the multiple-choice design question on 20t). Just: claim bd issue -> feature branch -> TDD (tests first) -> implement, making reasonable design calls consistent with existing patterns -> run full gate (pytest/ruff/basedpyright/codespell) -> /code-review -> finishing-a-development-branch (merge to main locally + push). State design decisions briefly in chat rather than asking. Heavy Workflow 2 ceremony (opsx delta spec, writing-plans) is NOT wanted for these one-endpoint tasks.

SCOPE DECISION (2026-06-03): forum endpoints are intentionally OUT OF SCOPE for python-gazelle. Gazelle exposes action=forum (main/viewforum/viewthread) for forum categories/threads/posts, but the user decided a tracker API client should NOT interact with the forum — the library covers torrent/music/account data only. Bead python-gazelle-e1q was closed WON'T-DO (not done). Do NOT add a ForumResource or forum models; do NOT re-propose forum coverage in coverage audits. If ever revisited, requires explicit user justification + a fresh issue. (Note: action=upload is also excluded but for a different reason — owned by Cross-Upload epic, not scope-rejected.)

OpenSpec maintenance convention (decided 2026-06-03): main specs in openspec/specs/ are maintained at CAPABILITY altitude, not per-field/per-endpoint. The lightweight endpoint-addition workflow (claim->branch->TDD->implement) intentionally SKIPS opsx delta-specs, so specs only need updating when a CAPABILITY changes (new resource namespace, new endpoint KIND, a new transport mode like the write path, or a changed cross-cutting approach) — NOT when fields/editions are added to existing models. To reconcile after drift: author an opsx change with delta specs (## ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED Requirements, each ### Requirement: with >=1 #### Scenario:), validate with `openspec validate --specs` / `openspec change validate <name>`, then `openspec archive <name> -y` (folds deltas into main specs, moves change to archive/ with date prefix). NOTE: archive only updates Requirements, NOT the `## Purpose` prose — fix Purpose paragraphs manually if they drift. First catch-up was change api-surface-expansion (2026-06-03). See also [[forum-endpoints-out-of-scope]].

Fixture field-coverage audit (2026-06): models drop many response fields via extra="ignore". Tracked as beads under epic python-gazelle-mca. (1) Artist (j0u): drops discography "torrentgroup" [different shape: groupId/groupName/groupYear, tags as {id:name} map, singular "torrent" key, extendedArtists — needs own model, NOT TorrentGroup], statistics {numGroups,numTorrents,numSeeders,numLeechers,numSnatches,numRequests}, similarArtists {artistId,name,score,similarId}, plus vanityHouse/bodyBbcode/requests. (2) Torrent (zvi): drops remasterYear/Title/RecordLabel/CatalogueNumber, description, logChecksum/logCount/ripLogIds, freeReason, reported. (3) TorrentGroup (d5f): drops releaseTypeName (have int release_type only), proxyImage, wikiBBcode, isBookmarked, time. (4) Browse (5gx): BrowseTorrent very thin — drops editionId, artists, remaster*, hasLog/logScore/hasCue/scene, per-edition snatches/seeders/leechers, freeleech flags isFreeleech/isNeutralLeech/isPersonalFreeleech/canUseToken/hasSnatched; TorrentResult drops cover/bookmarked/vanityHouse/groupTime/releaseType(str). (5) low-pri (rfb): group musicInfo credits (with/remixedBy/composers/conductor/dj/producer/arranger), notifications pagination meta currentPage/pages/numNew. Local fixtures cover artist/browse/index/notifications/torrent only — NO collage/torrentgroup fixtures.

Gazelle ajax route coverage audit (2026-06): core API client (epic ahf, closed) implements ~11 of ~29 documented OPSnet-fork actions (~38%). IMPLEMENTED: index, torrent, browse, artist, request, requests, collage, inbox, inbox&viewconv, notifications, download. MISSING READ: torrentgroup, user, usersearch, user_torrents, top10, bookmarks, subscriptions, forum(main/viewforum/viewthread), similar_artists, announcements, better, logchecker. MISSING WRITE: add_tag, add_log, request_fill (upload owned by cross-upload epic xn1). Follow-ups tracked under epic python-gazelle-mca. Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md. Transport currently only does read GETs + download — write endpoints need a POST/CSRF path first.

Tracker schema divergences (Orpheus vs RED) are handled by making the BASE pydantic models tolerant (Optional fields + union types like 'bool | str | None'), NOT by per-tracker subclasses. Reason: resources like TorrentResource.get always build the base model regardless of tracker, and tests/models validate both trackers' fixtures against the same base class. Example (python-gazelle-6ue): Torrent.info_hash/remastered made Optional (Orpheus omits), Torrent.free_torrent is 'bool | str | None' (Orpheus sends 'Normal' string enum, RED sends bool), Artist.tags is 'list[ArtistTag] | list[str]' (Orpheus sends {name,count} objects). Subclasses would be dead code unless the resource layer dispatches per tracker.
